Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or glomerular pathology. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with ketonuria and glucosuria, but blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, raising concern for possible renal glycosuria or other metabolic disturbances. Lipid profile shows hypercholesterolemia and high LDL. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal and metabolic status.
